description: Address review findings: fix HTML escaping bypass, remove entity strings from safeTags, reference MK_SYSTEM_PROMPT in sanitizer
steps: Step 1 - Fix HTML escaping bypass: Remove escapeForUser call from sendMessage in telegram.cpp so it becomes a pass-through. Instead, add an explicit escapeHtml call on the LLM reply in mk_entry.cpp right after the empty guard and before sendMessage. This ensures only LLM output gets escaped, while MK's own pre-formatted messages remain intact.
Step 2 - Remove entity strings from safeTags: Remove '<', '>', '&' from the safeTags vector in shouldEscapeHtml. Those are HTML entities, not tags.
Step 3 - Reference MK_SYSTEM_PROMPT in sanitizer: Replace the hardcoded sysPrompt string in sanitizeLLMResponse with a reference to the actual MK_SYSTEM_PROMPT constant.
acceptance_criteria: sendMessage no longer auto-escapes via escapeForUser
LLM reply in telegram_poll_loop is explicitly escaped before sendMessage
safeTags does not contain entity strings
sanitizeLLMResponse uses MK_SYSTEM_PROMPT directly instead of a hardcoded paraphrase
verification: Run 'cd /projects/sandbox/MK_os-2/MK_OS && make test' and confirm all tests pass
